page: Amazon Web Services r7gd.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor. Operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per core. The hardware configuration includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a top-tier rating in Geekbench multi-core testing with a score of 21157.0 and in software compilation with Clang. Passmark memory testing also placed this instance in the top tier, supported by strong memory bandwidth results, including 253.92 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 117.13 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng is rated as strong, though single-core performance in both stress-ng and Geekbench remains in the average tier.

The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, achieving 102.57 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a strong rating in Passmark database operations. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 76.73 GB/sec. However, text generation for large language models represents a relative bottleneck, with text generation speeds for a small 135M model rated as weak. Given the high memory density, dedicated physical cores, and local NVMe storage, this server is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores of the 2.6 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor on the arm64 architecture. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in Geekbench multi-core testing and Passmark memory scores, alongside strong multi-core CPU capabilities. The server excels in memory bandwidth and database operations, achieving 102.57 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. However, single-core processing and LLM text generation represent relative performance bottlenecks. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching systems, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving workloads requiring dedicated physical resources and high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 Graviton cores, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.metal is an arm64 bare-metal server with 64 Graviton cores, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An arm64 bare-metal server combining high memory density, dedicated physical cores, and local NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical cores on arm64 architecture
Equipped with 512.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Achieves strong database throughput, including 102.57 million Redis SET operations per second
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and software compilation
